seoTitle: How to Create Secondary Navigation in Squarespace
metaDescription: Add secondary navigation in Squarespace to organize your site's content better and help visitors find products, services, or posts faster.
intro: As a site grows to include more products, services, or resources, a single navigation menu can start to feel cluttered. This guide covers how to set up secondary navigation in Squarespace so visitors can find what they need more easily. It's especially useful for bloggers categorizing posts, small businesses highlighting services, or eCommerce brands organizing product categories.

steps.heading: Understand why secondary navigation matters
steps.body: Recognize how a secondary menu improves usability by organizing content that doesn't fit neatly into your primary navigation.

steps.heading: Set up the secondary navigation
steps.body: Follow the step-by-step process to add and configure secondary navigation on your Squarespace site, referencing the code shared in the pinned comment if needed.

steps.heading: Organize your content effectively
steps.body: Group related pages, products, or posts under the secondary menu so visitors can browse more intuitively.

steps.heading: Maintain and update the menu
steps.body: Revisit your secondary navigation periodically to keep it current as you add or remove content from your site.

steps.heading: Review the final navigation structure
steps.body: Check how the new menu looks and functions across your site to confirm it improves the overall browsing experience.

faq.question: What's the difference between primary and secondary navigation?
faq.answer: Primary navigation typically covers your main site sections, while secondary navigation organizes more specific content, like categories or subpages, underneath it.

faq.question: Does adding secondary navigation require custom code?
faq.answer: The tutorial references code available in the pinned comment, so some setup may involve adding custom code alongside Squarespace's built-in tools.

faq.question: Can secondary navigation update automatically as I add content?
faq.answer: Automation tools can be used to help keep navigation links current as your site changes, according to the video's guidance.

faq.question: Who benefits most from adding secondary navigation?
faq.answer: Bloggers, small business owners, and eCommerce brands with multiple categories of content tend to benefit most from a clearer navigation structure.
